summary: Iter 6 backend-only testing of the new localStorage-Bearer auth + reCAPTCHA v3 gating. 17/17 pytest tests pass. Verified: (1) /auth/register, /auth/login, /auth/google all return a non-empty `token` field in JSON; (2) Bearer-only auth (no cookie, fresh requests.Session) works for /auth/me, /properties, /dashboard/stats, /workspaces; (3) /auth/config exposes recaptcha_enabled=true and recaptcha_site_key when RECAPTCHA_* env is set; (4) /auth/register without recaptcha_token => 400 'Falta verificación reCAPTCHA'; with bogus token => 401 'reCAPTCHA inválido'; same for /auth/login; (5) /auth/google does NOT require recaptcha (bogus JWT => 401, not 400 for missing recaptcha); (6) full regression under Bearer auth: properties/units/tenants/reservations/fixed-expenses/dashboard/stats, bank CRUD+bulk, history close/months/year/delete, invoices/analyze reachable with both image(PNG) and PDF, workspaces invite/remove member, multi-tenant isolation (403 on cross-user X-Workspace-Id), 401 on all domain endpoints without token and on /auth/me with bogus token. .env was temporarily patched (RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY='') for the regression phase and fully restored with supervisor restart at the end (verified real keys back, /auth/config.recaptcha_enabled=true).

minor.issue: reCAPTCHA failure returns 401 for invalid token but 403 when score is below MIN_SCORE; frontend should treat both distinctly (currently only 400 is documented in spec).

reCAPTCHA verification is performed BEFORE email/password validation and duplicate-email check — on a 'missing recaptcha' 400 the server reveals no info about the form, which is fine, but if an attacker supplies a valid token they can still enumerate accounts via 401 vs 200 timing. Consider rate limiting.

When credential is malformed JWT, google-auth raises ValueError and we return 401 with error details in message ('Token de Google inválido: <stack-ish msg>'). OK for debug, but message may leak internal info. Consider sanitizing.

Still sets httpOnly cookie AND returns token in JSON. Works by design (cookie fallback) but double-state means /auth/logout only deletes cookie + current DB session; any token in localStorage persists on client until frontend clears it. The 401-interceptor in api.js handles this on the client, but server cannot invalidate all sessions of a user on password change (no endpoint to rotate/revoke all).
File remains monolithic — recommended to split into routers/ and services/. Non-blocking.
Returns google_client_id in plaintext (standard for GIS) and recaptcha_site_key (public by design). Fine, but no caching headers set; browsers will re-fetch on every login page load.

action_items: (Optional) Add server-side rate limiting on /auth/login and /auth/register to mitigate account enumeration (reCAPTCHA v3 alone is not enough at low scores).
(Optional) Sanitize the google_id_token exception message in /auth/google before returning in 401.
(Optional, reiterated) Split server.py into routers/services modules (still ~1280 lines).
(Optional) Add an endpoint to revoke all sessions of a user (e.g., on password change or explicit 'logout everywhere').
critical_code_review_comments: _verify_recaptcha correctly bypasses verification when RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY is empty, letting tests and dev runs work without Google. Good toggle.

_create_session now returns token in the JSON response so the frontend can store it in localStorage and send Authorization: Bearer — confirmed end-to-end with a fresh requests.Session that has zero cookies.
get_current_user accepts either Cookie or Authorization Bearer — tested both paths; Bearer-only works on every domain endpoint.
/auth/google is correctly NOT gated by reCAPTCHA (Google's own ID token verification is the proof-of-humanity).
CORS allow_origin_regex='.*' with allow_credentials=True is still required for cookie fallback; with Bearer this becomes less critical but preserved for backwards compat.
Multi-tenant isolation preserved under Bearer auth: B cannot list A's workspace properties (403) and does not see A's items in its own default workspace.
No _id leakage across any tested endpoint (deep-recursive check on properties, units, tenants, invoices, reservations, fixed-expenses, bank, history, dashboard, workspaces, auth responses).
updated_files: /app/backend/.env (temporarily during tests; restored to original at end — verified via /auth/config)
test_credentials: Dynamically registered TEST_iter6_*@example.com / Secret123! during tests; all cleaned up in Mongo afterwards. No shared admin account needed.
seed_data_creation: Tests register fresh users per run with TEST_iter6_ prefix. All users, sessions, workspaces, properties/units/tenants/invoices/reservations/fixed_expenses/bank_transactions/monthly_snapshots created are deleted in module-scoped fixture teardowns and a final sweep was done manually (0 TEST_ users remain).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 6 suite in /app/backend/tests/test_auth_iter6.py (17 tests) covers: recaptcha gating (Phase 1), token+Bearer-only (Phase 2 with RECAPTCHA_SECRET_KEY='' temporarily), full regression. Suite self-restores .env and restarts backend; safe to rerun. Iter 5 suite (/app/backend/tests/test_auth_iter5.py) still works for the non-recaptcha auth surface. To re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_auth_iter6.py -v. Real reCAPTCHA positive path still cannot be tested headless — only browser-driven flows can generate a valid v3 token. If frontend testing is requested, drive Login.jsx with Playwright and let reCAPTCHA v3 execute via window.grecaptcha.
rca of the issue: No issues to RCA — all review_request items pass: (a) token returned, (b) Bearer-only works, (c) /auth/config reports recaptcha_enabled+site_key, (d) register/login correctly reject missing/bogus recaptcha with 400/401, (e) /auth/google is not gated by recaptcha, (f) regression suite passes, (g) /auth/me still cleanly 401s without/with bad token.
